definition: A firmly held belief or opinion
example: I am not a consensus politician. I am a politician with conviction.

definition: General agreement
example: To me, consensus seems to be the process of abandoning all beliefs, principles, values, and policies.

definition: A place where a road meets or branches off another; a change of direction or course
example: The lady's not for turning.

definition: The transfer of a business, industry, or service from public to private ownership
example: Privatization brings efficiency and choice to the consumer.

definition: A project or undertaking, typically one that is difficult or requires effort; initiative and resourcefulness
example: The spirit of enterprise built this nation and will rebuild it.

definition: The political and economic policies of Margaret Thatcher
example: Thatcherism represented a fundamental shift in British politics.

definition: A strong, hard magnetic silvery-gray metal
example: They called her the Iron Lady for her resolve.

definition: Firm determination to do something
example: Our resolve shall not waver in the face of opposition.

definition: An act of selecting or making a decision when faced with two or more possibilities
example: Choice is the cornerstone of a free society.

definition: The state or fact of having a duty to deal with something or of having control over someone
example: With freedom comes responsibility.

definition: The act, state, or right of possessing something
example: The right to own property is fundamental to a free society.

definition: An area or arena in which commercial dealings are conducted; supply and demand
example: The free market creates prosperity for all who participate.

definition: Position, reputation, or status
example: Britain must reclaim its standing as a great power.

definition: A circular ornamental headdress worn by a monarch as a symbol of authority
example: The crown represents the dignity and continuity of the nation.

definition: A British overseas territory in the South Atlantic; subject of a 1982 war
example: The Falklands are British and will remain British.

definition: Courage in pain or adversity
example: Show fortitude in the defense of liberty.

definition: The quality or fact of being very determined; determination
example: Her tenacity in negotiations was legendary.

definition: The quality of being prudent; cautious with regard to practical matters
example: Prudence in policy is the hallmark of good government.

definition: Resolutely or dutifully firm and unwavering
example: Remain steadfast in your principles.

definition: Firmness of purpose; resoluteness
example: Determination and perseverance are the marks of true leadership.
